test_id_2.answer: {"headline_summary": "Current provider manual guidance says dual eligible pharmacy claims are limited by Medicare coordination rules. The policy is broadly consistent on Part D coverage, but the manual is more current and more specific about payment limits and enrollment requirements.", "aligned_pairs": [{"provider_manual": "For Medicare and Medicaid dual eligibles, the combined payment to the pharmacy cannot exceed the amount Medicaid would have paid for a Medicaid-only patient. test_id_31.query: What are the requirements for Coordination of Benefits (COB)?
test_id_31.answer: {"headline_summary": "For Coordination of Benefits, NYS Medicaid is the payer of last resort. Providers and pharmacies must bill any liable third party first and use all available coverage before billing Medicaid.", "aligned_pairs": [{"provider_manual": "If a member has an open workers' compensation case for a work-related injury, workers' compensation coverage must be used before NYRx Medicaid Pharmacy Program billing. If the claim is not related to the workers' compensation case, NYRx may be billed as primary with Eligibility Clarification Code 2 in field 309-C9. Medicaid is still the payer of last resort, and all other responsible payers must be included on the claim if applicable.", "policy_update": "Providers must bill applicable third parties before NYS Medicaid and use the proper primary payer. The policy is broader than the manual because it does not spell out the workers' compensation exception. It also says pharmacies must submit under the appropriate coverage benefit, medical or pharmacy."}, {"provider_manual": "All available resources must be used before Medicaid pays. A responsible third party must pay first, and Medicaid is the payer of last resort. Claims with other coverage may deny at point of service, and correctly submitted claims can receive patient responsibility or up to the Medicaid allowed amount for certain excluded drugs.", "policy_update": "Providers must bill applicable third parties before NYS Medicaid and exhaust existing benefits first. NYS Medicaid will pay the patient responsibility for correctly submitted coverable claims, up to the Medicaid allowed amount. The policy also instructs providers to ask about other third-party coverage and to coordinate with the proper primary payer."}], "similarities": ["Both sources say other liable coverage must be billed before Medicaid. [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines, New York State Medicaid Update April 2025 Volume 41 Number 4]"], "differences": ["The provider manual adds a specific workers' compensation rule and an Eligibility Clarification Code 2 detail, while the policy update states only the broader third-party billing requirement. [New York State Medicaid Program:"], "caveats": null, "policy_definition": [], "provider_manual_definition": []}

test_id_32.answer: {"headline_summary": "NYS Medicaid requires Coordination of Benefits by billing applicable third parties first and treating Medicaid as the payer of last resort. If a member has other coverage, the pharmacy must coordinate with the primary payer before billing Medicaid.", "aligned_pairs": [{"provider_manual": "For a work-related injury with an open Workers’ Compensation case, workers’ compensation coverage must be used before billing NYRx Medicaid Pharmacy Program. If the drug is not related to that Workers’ Compensation case, NYRx may be billed as the primary payer with Eligibility Clarification Code 2 in field 309-C9. Providers must also include all other responsible payers on the claim if applicable.", "policy_update": "NYS Medicaid requires pharmacies to coordinate with the proper primary payer before billing NYS Medicaid. NYS Medicaid is always the payer of last resort. test_id_60.answer: {"headline_summary": "Naloxone is reimbursed by NYS Medicaid when it is prescribed to a Medicaid member or dispensed under the State-issued naloxone standing order. The clearest billing detail is that OTC naloxone is submitted as a claim, and medication reimbursement uses NDC and the reimbursement methodology; if administration is billed separately, it uses procedure code 96372 and a separate claim.", "aligned_pairs": [{"provider_manual": "The provider manual does not give naloxone-specific reimbursement rules in the retrieved chunk. This makes the manual guidance broader than the naloxone policy.", "policy_update": "NY Medicaid covers over-the-counter naloxone when it is prescribed to a Medicaid member or dispensed under the State-issued naloxone standing order. The pharmacy must submit a claim and dispense it under a fiscal order or standing order. The drug is reimbursed when billed via NDC using reimbursement methodology. The administration claim uses procedure code 96372 and the pharmacy NPI in the Prescriber ID field.
